interestsIntro: I'm a computational biologist at the intersection of biology and AI — and I genuinely love it. There's something deeply meaningful about translating the complexity of living systems into models that can actually help us understand, predict, and intervene. I think this field is the future: the problems are hard, the data is rich, and the potential to accelerate discovery has never been greater. This page is a snapshot of where my curiosity tends to land.

projects.name: MolecuLead
projects.description: Full stack application connecting a biological knowledge graph, four bio-databases and an LLM via GraphRAG — enabling knowledge-grounded Q&A for scientific research in drug discovery.

projects.name: Boltz-RNA
projects.description: Fine-tuned a biomolecular foundation model for enhanced RNA structure prediction in a competition, scoring among the top 2% of participants.

projects.name: LeashDTI
projects.description: Graph Neural Networks for Drug-Target Interaction prediction — Kaggle-hosted competition.

classified.title: Harness Engineering for Platform Integrated Agents

engineering.title: 8x H100 Training
engineering.description: Training biomolecular and perturbation models on a single 8×H100 node — SLURM job scheduling, multi-GPU coordination, and memory-aware batching for large-scale fine-tuning runs.

engineering.title: Target Identification
engineering.description: Agentic workflows for target discovery — combining literature retrieval, knowledge graphs, and structured reasoning to surface and prioritise candidate targets for downstream validation.

engineering.title: Orchestrated Agent Workflows
engineering.description: End-to-end multi-agent pipelines for scientific and operational tasks — transcriptomics metadata curation, data cleaning, drug prioritisation, and literature synthesis, with tool use, tracing, and evals wired in.

engineering.title: Multi-Cloud Infrastructure
engineering.description: Production-grade cloud deployments across AWS, Azure, and GCP — IAM least-privilege, infrastructure-as-code, cost-aware scaling, and observability baked in from day one. Deepest experience on AWS; comfortable shipping and maintaining workloads on Azure and GCP as needed.

foundationModels.name: Boltz
foundationModels.description: A large-scale pre-trained transformer model optimized for protein folding trajectories. Boltz introduces a novel temporal attention mechanism that captures micro-second molecular dynamics with unprecedented fidelity.

foundationModels.name: Geneformer
foundationModels.description: Context-aware embeddings for single-cell transcriptomics. Geneformer leverages transfer learning to predict gene network perturbations in rare disease states.

foundationModels.name: GEARS
foundationModels.description: Graph Ensemble of Allelic Response Systems. A specialized GNN framework for modeling multi-gene interactions and epistatic effects in synthetic biology.

foundationModels.name: STATE
foundationModels.description: Arc Institute's foundation model for virtual cell simulation — trained on massive perturbation atlases to predict transcriptional and phenotypic responses to genetic and chemical interventions across cell types.
